overview: This week I focused on building responsible AI guardrails into my SAFe Feature Spec System from a product and engineering perspective. Together with Claude, I created 9 new evaluation modules, a 4-section Responsible AI dashboard, and a reusable governance template. The process followed a deliberate arc: audit what I had, fill the gaps, validate with data, and generalize the patterns.

keyPoints.title: Initial audit revealed only 48% responsible AI coverage across 25 checks
keyPoints.description: When I ran the Responsible AI Checker against my SAFe Feature System, it passed just 12 of 25 guidelines, with gaps clustered in cost governance, audit trails, content safety, and regression baselines.

keyPoints.title: Reviewer agent consumes ~80% of total pipeline input tokens
keyPoints.description: The infrastructure auditor surfaced that the Reviewer agent — not the Generator — dominates token usage because it receives the full spec, full rubric, and system prompt as input.

keyPoints.title: Claude Code proactively improved my two-cap CostGuard design
keyPoints.description: The original directive implied per-pass and per-run cost limits but didn't operationalize how to select between them; Claude Code detected this gap and added a context parameter to enforce the correct cap at call time.

keyPoints.title: Every boosted case returned 0 invented claims; every bare case had at least one
keyPoints.description: Across all 6 golden set cases, the grounding checker found that PM context boosts eliminated inventions entirely, providing the clearest quantitative signal yet for why richer input matters.

keyPoints.title: Same content produced different grounding verdicts on consecutive runs
keyPoints.description: For web_001_bare, a batch run classified 12 Generator claims as Inventions, while a drill-down run on identical content reclassified them all as Extrapolations, highlighting the need for server-side verdict logic.

keyPoints.title: Live bias detection flagged WEBPAGE mean at 77.0 vs. 86.6 for other feature types
keyPoints.description: The bias detection script grouping eval runs by feature type surfaced a meaningful scoring gap exceeding the default 10-point threshold between WEBPAGE and other feature categories.
keyPoints.sectionName: Day 4: Bias Detection + Responsible AI Dashboard

keyPoints.title: Audit trail CLI trace viewer saved real debugging time across Days 3 and 4
keyPoints.description: The 7-event-type audit trail and its terminal trace viewer proved more valuable than expected, letting me inspect the full decision sequence while debugging grounding checker behavior without leaving the terminal.
